request.description:
Resend `OTP`

If the `OTP` expires, this route is use to get a new one.

The `token` of the expired `OTP` is required as the `authentication`

Verify the OTP

This route is used to validate and verify the `otp` sent.

The `token` of the `OTP` is required as the `authentication`

Allow user to create account

`Business account registration does not require the ref` filed.

`ref`: is gotten from the invitation sent with the `Send Invite` endpoint. The invitation is verified using the `Verify Invite` endpoint, which sends `OTP` to the invitation email. The `OTP` is verified with `Verify OTP` endpoint and upon success routes to registration page.

Allow user to create account

The `token` from the `verify` route is required as the `authentication`
Allow user to log into the application
Allow user to login into the application after the 2FA OTP has been verified successfully.
Allow user to update 2FA status
Allow user to gernerate new `accesToken` and `refreshToken` when the `accessToken` expires.

### Auth Check

This endpoint is used to verify the authentication status and retrieve user information.

**Request Body**
- No request body parameters required.

**Response**
- Status (200 OK)
- Content Type: application/json
- Body:
    ```json
    {
        "success": true,
        "msg": "",
        "user": {
            "userId": "",
            "username": "",
            "firstName": "",
            "lastName": "",
            "email": "",
            "phoneNumber": "",
            "status": "",
            "type": "",
            "role": "",
            "state": "",
            "verified": true,
            "balance": 0,
            "mFA": true,
            "picture": [],
            "createdAt": "",
            "updatedAt": ""
        }
    }
    ```

**Example Response**
```json
{
    "success": true,
    "msg": "",
    "user": {
        "userId": "",
        "username": "",
        "firstName": "",
        "lastName": "",
        "email": "",
        "phoneNumber": "",
        "status": "",
        "type": "",
        "role": "",
        "state": "",
        "verified": true,
        "balance": 0,
        "mFA": true,
        "picture": [],
        "createdAt": "",
        "updatedAt": ""
    }
}
```

request.description:
### Auth Confirm Password

This endpoint is used to confirm the user's password.

**Note**

supply the `token` value as `password_token` field value in the header of the original request.  
The token expires in `3 minutes`

#### Request Body

- `password` (string, required): The user's password.
    

#### Response

- `success` (boolean): Indicates if the password confirmation was successful.
    
- `msg` (string): A message related to the password confirmation.
    
- `token` (string): The token associated with the password confirmation.
    

Example:

``` json
{
    "success": true,
    "msg": "",
    "token": ""
}

 ```

item.description:
This provides guide on user initial activities to get registered or create account. The steps to `sign-up` are as follows:

1. `otp` is sent to email using `send-otp` endpoint.
    
2. The `otp` and `token` from `step 1` is provided to the `verify-otp` endpoint for `verification` and `validation`
    
3. The `token` received from `step 2` respond is then used to process the registration through `registeration` endpoint.

request.description:
This endpoint allows administrators to update a store category by sending an HTTP PATCH request to {{baseUrl}}/admin/store/store_category?search=ROBXSNT. The request should include a JSON payload in the raw request body type with the keys "parentId" and "name".

### Request Body
- `parentId`: (text) The ID of the parent category.
- `name`: (text) The updated name of the category.

### Response
Upon successful execution, the endpoint returns a JSON response with a status code of 200 and the following content:
```json
{
    "success": true,
    "msg": ""
}
```
The "success" field indicates whether the update was successful, and the "msg" field provides any additional information related to the update.

### Admin Invitation Endpoint

This endpoint is used to send an invitation to a user for admin access.

#### Request Body

- `email` (string, required): The email address of the user to whom the invitation will be sent.
    
- `role` (string, required): The role assigned to the user upon accepting the invitation.
    

#### Response

The response for this request is in XML format and does not contain any specific data. To document the response as a JSON schema, the response data should be provided by the API.

### Delete Admin Invitation

This endpoint is used to delete an invitation for admin access using the email parameter.

#### Request Parameters

- email (query parameter) : The email address for which the admin invitation needs to be deleted.
    

#### Response

- Status: 200
    
- Content-Type: application/json
    

``` json
{
    "success": true,
    "msg": ""
}

 ```

The response indicates the success of the deletion operation, with a "success" boolean flag and an optional "msg" field for additional information.

request.description:
The endpoint retrieves business account information for admin users. It accepts a search parameter to filter the results.

### Response

The response will be a JSON object with the following properties:

- `success` (boolean): Indicates if the request was successful.
    
- `msg` (string): A message related to the request, if any.
    
- `account` (array): An array of objects containing business account details with the following properties:
    - `userId` (string): The unique identifier of the user.
        
    - `username` (string): The username of the user.
        
    - `firstName` (string): The first name of the user.
        
    - `lastName` (string): The last name of the user.
        
    - `email` (string): The email address of the user.
        
    - `phoneNumber` (string): The phone number of the user.
        
    - `status` (string): The status of the account.
        
    - `type` (string): The type of the account.
        
    - `role` (string): The role of the user.
        
    - `state` (string): The state of the account.
        
    - `verified` (boolean): Indicates if the account is verified.
        
    - `balance` (number): The balance in the account.
        
    - `mFA` (boolean): Indicates if multi-factor authentication is enabled for the account.
        
    - `picture` (array): An array of pictures associated with the account.
        
    - `createdAt` (string): The date and time when the account was created.
        
    - `updatedAt` (string): The date and time when the account was last updated.

### Update Admin Account

This endpoint is used to update the state of an account by admin.

#### Request Bodys

- `state`: The new state for the account. Possible values are "activate" or "deactivate".
    
- `userId`: The ID of the user whose account state needs to be updated.
    

#### Response Body

The response will contain the updated details of the admin account.

request.description:
### Get All Admin Tickets

This endpoint retrieves all the admin tickets.

#### Request Body

This is a GET request and does not require a request body.

#### Response Body

- `success` (boolean): Indicates if the request was successful.
    
- `msg` (string): A message related to the request.
    
- `tickets` (array): An array of ticket objects containing the following fields:
    
    - `viewed` (boolean): Indicates if the ticket has been viewed.
        
    - `id` (string): The unique identifier of the ticket.
        
    - `sender` (object): Contains information about the sender, including `firstName`, `lastName`, and `type`.
        
    - `title` (string): The title of the ticket.
        
    - `description` (string): The description of the ticket.
        
    - `status` (string): The status of the ticket.
        
    - `priority` (string): The priority of the ticket.
        
    - `createdAt` (string): The timestamp when the ticket was created.
        
    - `updatedAt` (string): The timestamp when the ticket was last updated.

request.description:
This endpoint allows administrators to update a ticket. The request should include the ticket ID and/or status priority, or viewed status in the raw request body.

### Request Body

- `id` (string): The unique identifier of the ticket to be updated.
    
- `status` (string): The updated status of the ticket (e.g., open, closed, in-progress).
    
- `priority` (string): The updated priority of the ticket (e.g., low, medium, high).
    
- `viewed` (boolean): Indicates whether the ticket has been viewed.
    

### Response

Upon a successful update, the endpoint returns a JSON response with a status code of 200 and the following content:

``` json
{
  "success": true,
  "msg": ""
}

 ```

- `success` (boolean): Indicates whether the update was successful.
    
- `msg` (string): An optional message related to the update.

### Update Read Status of Ticket Chat

This endpoint is used to update the read status of a ticket chat in the admin interface.

#### Request Body

- `ticketId` (string) - The ID of the ticket for which the chat read status is to be updated.
    
- `chatId` (array) - An array of chat IDs for which the read status is to be updated.
    

#### Response

Upon successful execution, the API returns a JSON object with the following fields:

- `success` (boolean) - Indicates whether the operation was successful.
    
- `msg` (string) - A message indicating the result of the operation.
    

Example:

``` json
{
    "success": true,
    "msg": ""
}

 ```

request.description:
### Add New FAQ

This endpoint allows the addition of a new FAQ.

#### Request Body

- `title` (string) - The title of the FAQ.
    
- `description` (string) - The description of the FAQ.
    
- `category` (string) - The FAQ category.
    

Target values:

`vendors`

`riders`

`shopper`

`all`  

Example:

``` json
{
    "title": "Sample FAQ Title",
    "description": "Sample FAQ Description",
    "category": "Sample Category"
}

 ```

#### Response

Upon successful addition, the endpoint returns a JSON object with the following fields:

- `success` (boolean) - Indicates if the operation was successful.
    
- `msg` (string) - A message indicating the result of the operation.
    

Example:

``` json
{
    "success": true,
    "msg": ""
}

 ```

request.description:
This endpoint deletes a frequently asked question (FAQ) with a specific ID. The ID of the FAQ to be deleted is provided as a query parameter in the URL.

### Request Parameters

- id (query parameter): The unique identifier of the FAQ to be deleted.
    

### Response

Upon a successful deletion, the endpoint returns a JSON response with a status code of 200 and a content type of application/json. The response body contains the following fields:

- success (boolean): Indicates whether the deletion was successful.
    
- msg (string): An optional message related to the deletion process.
    

#### Example Response

``` json
{
    "success": true,
    "msg": ""
}

 ```

### Get FAQ by ID

This endpoint retrieves the FAQ details based on the provided ID.

#### Request

- Method: GET
    
- URL: {{baseUrl}}/admin/faq/:id
    

#### Response

- Status: 200
    
- Content-Type: application/json
    

``` json
{
    "success": true,
    "msg": "",
    "faq": {
        "id": "",
        "title": "",
        "description": "",
        "category": "",
        "account": {
            "userId": "",
            "firstName": "",
            "lastName": "",
            "type": "",
            "role": ""
        },
        "createdAt": "",
        "updatedAt": ""
    }
}

 ```

- `msg` (string): A message related to the update.
    

#### Example Response

``` json
  "success": true,
  "msg": ""
}

 ```

The KYC documents are:

### Retrieve KYC Information

This endpoint makes an HTTP GET request to retrieve Know Your Customer (KYC) information for a business.

#### Request Body

This request does not require a request body.

#### Response Body

- `success` (boolean): Indicates if the request was successful.
    
- `msg` (string): A message related to the request, if any.
    
- `kyc` (object): The KYC information for the business.
    - `userId` (string): The user ID associated with the KYC information.
        
    - `profile` (array): An array of profiles associated with the business.
        - `storeId` (string): The store ID.
            
        - `address` (object): The address details for the store.
            - `state` (string): The state of the store address.
                
            - `city` (string): The city of the store address.
                
            - `lga` (string): The Local Government Area of the store address.
                
            - `town` (string): The town of the store address.
                
            - `street` (string): The street of the store address.
                
            - `houseNo` (string): The house number of the store address.
                
            - `landMark` (string): The landmark near the store address.
                
        - `banner` (object): The banner details for the store.
            - `id` (string): The ID of the banner.
                
            - `url` (string): The URL of the banner image.
                
        - `logo` (object): The logo details for the store.
            - `id` (string): The ID of the logo.
                
            - `url` (string): The URL of the logo image.
                
    - `bankDetails` (array): An array of bank details associated with the business.
        
    - `documents` (array): An array of documents associated with the business.
        
    - `store` (array): An array of stores associated with the business.
        - `storeId` (string): The store ID.
            
        - `name` (string): The name of the store.
            
        - `category` (array): An array of categories associated with the store.
            - `_id` (string): The ID of the category.
                
            - `id` (string): The ID of the category.
                
            - `name` (string): The name of the category.
                
            - `parent` (string): The parent category of the category.
                
            - `description` (string): The description of the category.
                
            - `status` (string): The status of the category.
                
        - `createdAt` (string): The creation date of the store.
            
        - `updatedAt` (string): The last update date of the store.
            
    - `logistics` (array): An array of logistics associated with the business.
        
    - `onBoarded` (boolean): Indicates if the business is onboarded.

request.description:
### PATCH /business/kyc/finalize

This endpoint is used to finalize the KYC (Know Your Customer) process for a business.

#### Request Body

- `storeId` (string): The unique identifier of the store.
    
- `tin` (string, optional): The Tax Identification Number (TIN) of the business.
    

#### Response

The response is in JSON format with the following schema:

``` json
{
  "success": boolean,
  "msg": string
}

 ```

- `success` (boolean): Indicates whether the KYC finalization was successful.
    
- `msg` (string): A message providing additional information about the operation.
    

#### Example

Request:

``` json
{
  "storeId": "W2QUI8V48L98Q6I",
  "tin": ""
}

 ```

Response:

``` json
{
  "success": true,
  "msg": ""
}

 ```

request.description:
## Update Order Status

This endpoint allows you to update the status of a specific order identified by its `orderId`. The request modifies the current status of the order in the system.

`Order Statuses:`

``` json
 pending: "pending",
    pickup: "pickup",
    inProcess: "inProcess",
    delivered: "delivered",
    cancelled: "cancelled",
    draft: "draft",
    ready: "ready",
    completed: "completed",
    failed: "failed",
    open: "open",
    closed: "closed",

 ```

### Request

- **Method**: PATCH
    
- **URL**: `{{baseUrl}}/order/store/update/:orderId`
    

#### Path Parameters

- `orderId` (string): The unique identifier of the order that you want to update.
    

#### Request Body

The request body must be in JSON format and should contain the following key:

- `status` (string): The new status to be assigned to the order. For example, it could be values like "open", "closed", etc.
    

**Example Request Body:**

``` json
{
  "status": "open"
}

 ```

### Response

The response will return a status code indicating the result of the operation.

- **Status Code**: 400
    
- **Content-Type**: application/json
    

#### Response Body

In case of an error, the response will contain an error message in JSON format:

``` json
{
  "error": ""
}

 ```

### Notes

- Ensure that the `orderId` is valid and corresponds to an existing order in the system.
    
- The `status` value must be acceptable by the system; otherwise, a 400 error will be returned.
    
- This endpoint is useful for managing the lifecycle of orders by allowing updates to their statuses dynamically.

This endpoint allows the user to create a new product by providing the necessary details.

#### Form-Data Parameters

- `brand` (text): The brand of the product.
    
- `price` (text): The price of the product.

- `discountCode` (text): The code used to claim the discount.
    
- `additionalInfo` (text): More information about the product.
    
- `quantity` (text): The number of products.
    
- `variation` (text): An array of objects representing product variations.
    
- `tags` (text): Tags associated with the product.
    
- `weight` (text): The weight of the product.
    
- `sku` (text): An optional value for product identification.
    

#### Response

The response is a JSON object with the following schema:

``` json
{
    "type": "object",
    "properties": {
        "success": {
 ```

- `prodId` (string) - The product ID.
    
- `storeId` (string) - The store ID.
    
- `store` (object) - Details about the store, including `storeId` and `name`.
    
- `title` (string) - The title of the store product.
    
- `description` (string) - The description of the store product.
    
- `media` (array) - An array of media objects, including `mainImage` and `others` with their respective `id` and `url`.

- `subcategory` (string) - The subcategory of the store product.
    
- `brand` (string) - The brand of the store product.
    
- `tags` (array) - An array of tags associated with the store product.
    
- `quantity` (number) - The quantity of the store product.
    
- `weight` (string) - The weight of the store product.
    
- `rating` (number) - The rating of the store product.
    
- `likes` (number) - The number of likes for the store product.
    
- `additionalInfo` (array) - Additional information about the store product.
    
- `barcode` (array) - An array of barcodes associated with the store product.

The request body must be in JSON format and include the following parameters:

- **items** (Array of Objects): A list of items to be ordered.
    
    - **prodId** (String): The unique identifier for the product.
        
    - **name** (String): The name of the product.
        
    - **quantity** (Integer): The quantity of the product being ordered.
        
    - **price** (Integer): The price of the product.
        
- **type** (String): The type of order, e.g., "delivery".
    
- **destinationAddress** (Object): The address where the order will be delivered.
    
    - **userId** (String): The unique identifier for the user.
        
    - **addressId** (String): The unique identifier for the address.
        
    - **title** (String): The title or name associated with the address.
        
    - **phoneNumber** (String): Contact phone number for delivery.
        
    - **street** (String): The street address.
        
    - **houseNumber** (String): The house number.
        
    - **city** (String): The city of the delivery address.
        
    - **state** (String): The state of the delivery address.
        
- **storeId** (String): The unique identifier for the store from which the order is being placed.
    
- **paymentType** (String): The method of payment, e.g., "card".
    
- **cardDetails** (Object): The payment card information.
    
    - **name** (String): The name on the card.
        
    - **cardNumber** (String): The card number.
        
    - **expiryDate** (String): The expiration date of the card.
        
    - **cvv** (String): The CVV code of the card.
        
    - **pin** (String): The PIN for the card.
        
    - **cardType** (String): The type of card (e.g., "Mastercard").
        
    - **saveCard** (String): Indicates whether to save the card details for future use.
        
- **discount** (Integer): Any discount applied to the order.
    
- **subTotal** (Integer): The subtotal amount for the order before taxes and discounts.
    
- **total** (Integer): The total amount for the order after applying discounts.
    

#### Expected Response

Upon successful order initialization, the response will be in JSON format with the following structure:

- **status** (String): Status of the request.
    
- **message** (String): A message providing additional information about the request.
    
- **data** (Object): Contains the following details:
    
    - **orderId** (String): The unique identifier for the newly created order.
        
    - **token** (String): A token associated with the order.
        

#### Response Status

- **201 Created**: Indicates that the order has been successfully initialized.

request.description:
## Fund Wallet API

This endpoint allows users to fund their wallet using a specified payment method. In this case, the payment type is set to "card", and the user must provide the necessary card details along with the amount to be funded.

### Request

**HTTP Method:** POST  
**Endpoint:** `{{baseUrl}}/transaction/wallet/fund_wallet`

#### Request Body Parameters

- **paymentType** (string): The type of payment being used. Currently, the supported type is "card".
    
- **cardDetails** (object): An object containing the details of the card being used for the transaction.
    
    - **name** (string): The name on the card.
        
    - **cardNumber** (string): The card number (16 digits).
        
    - **expiryDate** (string): The expiry date of the card in MM/YY format.
        
    - **cvv** (string): The card verification value (CVV).
        
    - **cardType** (string): The type of card (e.g., Mastercard, Visa).
        
    - **saveCard** (string): Indicates whether to save the card information for future transactions (e.g., "yes" or "no").
        
- **amount** (integer): The amount to be funded to the wallet, specified in the smallest currency unit (e.g., cents).
    

### Response

**Status Code:** 200  
**Content-Type:** application/json

#### Response Body

- **status** (string): The status of the request (e.g., success or failure).
    
- **msg** (string): A message providing additional information about the request status.
    
- **data** (object): An object containing transaction details.
    
    - **authorization_url** (string): The URL for authorization, if applicable.
        
    - **access_code** (string): The access code for the transaction.
        
    - **reference** (string): A reference number for tracking the transaction.
        

### Notes

- Ensure that all card details are accurate to avoid transaction failures.
    
- The `saveCard` parameter should be used with caution, as it involves storing sensitive card information.
    
- The response will provide essential details for further processing or tracking of the transaction.

### Get Wallet Balance

This endpoint retrieves the current balance of the user's wallet.

- **Method:** GET
    
- **Endpoint:** `{{baseUrl}}/transaction/wallet/balance`
    

#### Response

On a successful request, the server will respond with a status code of `200` and a JSON object containing the following structure:

- **success**: A boolean value indicating whether the request was successful.
    
- **msg**: A string that may contain additional information or messages (will be empty in this case).
    
- **data**: An object containing the wallet balance information.
    
    - **balance**: A numeric value representing the current balance in the wallet.
        

#### Example Response

``` json
{
  "success": true,
  "msg": "",
  "data": {
    "balance": 0
  }
}

 ```

### Usage

To use this endpoint, simply send a GET request to the specified URL. The response will provide you with the current balance of your wallet, allowing you to check your available funds easily.

### Retrieve KYC Information

This endpoint makes an HTTP GET request to retrieve Know Your Customer (KYC) information for a business.

#### Request Body

This request does not require a request body.

#### Response Body

- `success` (boolean): Indicates if the request was successful.
    
- `msg` (string): A message related to the request, if any.
    
- `kyc` (object): The KYC information for the business.
    - `userId` (string): The user ID associated with the KYC information.
        
    - `profile` (array): An array of profiles associated with the business.
        - `storeId` (string): The store ID.
            
        - `address` (object): The address details for the store.
            - `state` (string): The state of the store address.
                
            - `city` (string): The city of the store address.
                
            - `lga` (string): The Local Government Area of the store address.
                
            - `town` (string): The town of the store address.
                
            - `street` (string): The street of the store address.
                
            - `houseNo` (string): The house number of the store address.
                
            - `landMark` (string): The landmark near the store address.
                
        - `banner` (object): The banner details for the store.
            - `id` (string): The ID of the banner.
                
            - `url` (string): The URL of the banner image.
                
        - `logo` (object): The logo details for the store.
            - `id` (string): The ID of the logo.
                
            - `url` (string): The URL of the logo image.
                
    - `bankDetails` (array): An array of bank details associated with the business.
        
    - `documents` (array): An array of documents associated with the business.
        
    - `store` (array): An array of stores associated with the business.
        - `storeId` (string): The store ID.
            
        - `name` (string): The name of the store.
            
        - `category` (array): An array of categories associated with the store.
            - `_id` (string): The ID of the category.
                
            - `id` (string): The ID of the category.
                
            - `name` (string): The name of the category.
                
            - `parent` (string): The parent category of the category.
                
            - `description` (string): The description of the category.
                
            - `status` (string): The status of the category.
                
        - `createdAt` (string): The creation date of the store.
            
        - `updatedAt` (string): The last update date of the store.
            
    - `logistics` (array): An array of logistics associated with the business.
        
    - `onBoarded` (boolean): Indicates if the business is onboarded.

### Get FAQ

The GET request is used to retrieve a list of frequently asked questions (FAQ).

#### Request Body

This request does not require a request body.

#### Response Body

The response will be in JSON format and will contain the following fields:

- `success` (boolean) - Indicates whether the request was successful.
    
- `msg` (string) - A message related to the success or failure of the request.
    
- `faq` (array) - An array of FAQ items, each containing the following fields:
    
    - `id` (string) - The unique identifier of the FAQ item.

    - `category` (string) - The category of the FAQ item.
        
    - `account` (string) - The account related to the FAQ item.
        
    - `updatedAt` (string) - The date and time when the FAQ item was last updated.
        
- `count` (integer) - The total count of FAQ items returned in the response.
    

An example response body:

``` json
{
    "success": true,
    "msg": "",
    "faq": [
        {
            "id": "",
            "title": "",
            "description": "",
            "category": "",
            "account": "",
            "updatedAt": ""

}
 ```
